column1: # World Bank World Development Indicators (WDI) indicator catalog
# Source: World Bank WDI v2 API
# License: CC BY 4.0 International per the World Bank Terms of Use for Datasets
# Citation: World Bank. 2024. World Development Indicators. Washington
# This file is the single source of truth for which WDI indicators are read
# in Stage 2. Every Stage 2 WDI adapter call resolves its raw_column from
# this file. Adding/removing an indicator here is the only change needed to
# widen or narrow the WDI ingestion for the prototype.
# Scale conventions used in this catalog:
#   index_0_1          — normalized indices (e.g. Gini on 0–1 scale)
#   ratio              — dimensionless ratios (e.g. school enrollment ratio)
# All 14 indicators normalize to 0-1 in Stage 5 (the normalized_scale_target
# column is documentation for the score modules
# NOTE on indicator codes: WDI v2 uses NE.EXP.GNFS.ZS and NE.IMP.GNFS.ZS
# The raw_column values below reflect the actual WDI v2 API codes.
# NOTE on NV.IMP.TOTL.ZS direction: marked higher_is_better=True for the
# prototype (import capacity correlates with economic health). The Phase D
# score module may flip this if chronic trade deficits are interpreted as
# adverse. Confirm with user or defer to score module.

column8: Total population; denominator for per-capita metrics and country_years.population
GDP at market prices in current USD; country_years.gdp_current_usd column
GDP per capita (market exchange rate); headline economic well-being metric
GDP at constant 2015 USD; inflation-adjusted for cross-year comparison
GDP per capita at PPP (constant 2017 international $); cross-country purchasing-power metric
GNI per capita (Atlas method); World Bank lending-classification denominator
Exports of goods and services as share of GDP; trade openness signal
Imports of goods and services as share of GDP; open-economy signal (see catalog header note on direction)
Adult literacy rate; coverage begins 1990 for most countries
Secondary school enrollment (gross %); can exceed 100 due to over-age students
Under-5 mortality rate; inverse direction (lower = better)
Gini index of income inequality; inverse direction (lower = less inequality)
